Whole app is a true firewall (blocks eve: Whole app is a true firewall (blocks even the page). API only blocks data/sign-in/settings but lets the page shell load — it can never hard-brick the server.
Blocks every request, including the page: Blocks every request, including the page itself — a true firewall. Loopback and the safe-list are still exempt so you can recover.
Blocks the HTTP API only (data, sign-in,: Blocks the HTTP API only (data, sign-in, settings). The page shell still loads but does nothing for a blocked IP. Cannot hard-brick the server.
The guard is disabled. No request is che: The guard is disabled. No request is checked.
Requests are checked and would-be blocks: Requests are checked and would-be blocks are logged, but nothing is actually blocked. Use it to preview your rules safely.
Every IP is allowed except those in the: Every IP is allowed except those in the block-list below.
Only IPs in the allow-list (and the safe: Only IPs in the allow-list (and the safe-list) may reach the API. Everything else is blocked.
One entry per line. Accepts a single IP,: One entry per line. Accepts a single IP, a CIDR block (10.0.0.0/8) or a start-end range (1.2.3.4-1.2.3.9). IPv4 and IPv6. Lines starting with # are comments.
The raw socket address differs from the: The raw socket address differs from the forwarded one because trust-proxy is on.
With the current rules, your IP is allow: With the current rules, your IP is allowed.
With the current rules, your IP would be: With the current rules, your IP would be blocked.
This configuration would block your own: This configuration would block your own IP
Add your IP to the allow-list or the saf: Add your IP to the allow-list or the safe-list before saving, or you may lose access to the app from this address.
Choose Monitor, Block-list or Allow-list: Choose Monitor, Block-list or Allow-list to configure rules.
Applies in every mode and is never block: Applies in every mode and is never blocked. Put trusted admin IPs here so you cannot lock yourself out.
Keeps local and CLI access working — rec: Keeps local and CLI access working — recommended to leave on.
When your app sits behind Nginx / a load: When your app sits behind Nginx / a load balancer / Cloudflare, the real client IP is in a forwarded header. Turn OFF only for a direct connection — a forwarded header can be spoofed by clients that reach the server directly.
Verbose — this fires on every API call. Use briefly for debugging only.
Test an IP against the current (unsaved): Test an IP against the current (unsaved) rules
